MPV Android native assets
=========================

Source:
- Reference code: https://github.com/FongMi/mpv-android
- Native binary baseline: https://github.com/mpv-android/mpv-android/releases/tag/2026-04-25
- Vulkan native refresh: https://github.com/marlboro-advance/mpvEx
  - app/libs/mpv-android-lib-v0.0.1.aar

Downloaded APKs:
- https://github.com/mpv-android/mpv-android/releases/download/2026-04-25/app-default-arm64-v8a-release.apk
  SHA-256: 4400bcba6be9cec1128e24d1eba153d8727384926b0639fa7fe44d4e36b04f81
- https://github.com/mpv-android/mpv-android/releases/download/2026-04-25/app-default-armeabi-v7a-release.apk
  SHA-256: 95a863eddc407f95bb1e5917957d7a9b0e6e290109ee5709b7eb3bc90458a024

Bundled files:
- app/src/arm64_v8a/assets/mpv-libs/arm64-v8a/*.so
- app/src/armeabi_v7a/assets/mpv-libs/armeabi-v7a/*.so
- app/src/main/assets/cacert.pem

Current native split:
- libplayer.so is still built from third_party/mpv-player-jni so this project
  keeps the structured END_FILE reason/error bridge.
- libmpv.so and its FFmpeg/libc++ dependencies are refreshed from mpvEx's
  mpv-android-lib-v0.0.1.aar because that build enables libplacebo Vulkan.
- The refreshed libmpv.so feature list contains "vulkan" and the Android
  Vulkan context string "androidvk".

Packaging note:
The project already depends on nextlib-media3ext, whose AAR contains libavcodec.so,
libavutil.so, libswresample.so and libswscale.so. MPV libraries are therefore
stored as assets and loaded from the app private directory at runtime instead of
being packaged through jniLibs.

The MPV FFmpeg dependency names are patched in-place with equal-length names to
avoid Android linker reuse of nextlib's libraries:
- libavcodec.so -> libmvcodec.so
- libavdevice.so -> libmvdevice.so
- libavfilter.so -> libmvfilter.so
- libavformat.so -> libmvformat.so
- libavutil.so -> libmvutil.so
- libswresample.so -> libmwresample.so
- libswscale.so -> libmwscale.so

After importing external MPV/FFmpeg binaries, verify with NDK llvm-readelf that
DT_NEEDED uses the renamed libraries and does not point to libav*.so/libsw*.so.
